checks.label: Abnormal Reboot Count
checks.description: High reboot count (>500) may indicate instability, tampering, or crash loops

checks.label: Recent System Crashes
checks.description: Excessive system crashes may indicate exploitation attempts or instability

checks.label: ANR Events Count
checks.description: High ANR count may indicate resource exhaustion or DoS conditions

checks.label: Kernel Panic History
checks.description: Kernel panics may indicate kernel exploitation or hardware tampering

checks.label: Native Crash Tombstones
checks.description: Native crashes can indicate buffer overflow or memory corruption exploits

checks.label: Clipboard Sensitive Data
checks.description: Clipboard contains sensitive keywords (passwords, tokens, credentials)

checks.label: Suspicious Logcat Keywords
checks.description: Logcat contains suspicious exploitation-related keywords

checks.label: Device Uptime Analysis
checks.command: cat /proc/uptime 2>/dev/null | awk '{days=int($1/86400); if(days>365) print "LONG_UPTIME_"days"_DAYS"; else if(days<1) print "RECENT_REBOOT"; else print "NORMAL_"days"_DAYS"}'
checks.description: Very long uptime (>1yr) or recent reboot may indicate anomaly

checks.label: SELinux Denial Spike
checks.description: High SELinux denial count (>100) may indicate privilege escalation attempts

checks.label: Temp Directory Artifacts
checks.description: Suspicious files in /data/local/tmp may indicate active exploitation

checks.label: Corporate MDM Accounts
checks.description: Enterprise accounts detected - device may be under MDM management

checks.label: Email Accounts Count
checks.description: Number of email accounts - more accounts increase attack surface

checks.label: Multi-User Profiles Count
checks.description: Multiple user profiles may have inconsistent security configurations

checks.label: Work Profile Present
checks.command: dumpsys user 2>/dev/null | grep -ci 'work profile\|managed profile' || echo 0
checks.description: Work profile provides data separation but grants MDM access

checks.label: Guest Mode Enabled
checks.description: Guest mode allows unauthenticated temporary device access

checks.label: Secondary User Profiles
checks.description: Secondary users may bypass primary user security policies

checks.label: Background Data Whitelist Apps
checks.command: cmd netpolicy list restrict-background-whitelist 2>/dev/null | wc -l || echo 0
checks.description: Apps whitelisted for background data may exfiltrate data silently

checks.label: Active VoIP Telecom Calls
checks.description: Active calls detected - avoid sensitive discussion during audit

checks.label: Data Usage Anomaly Check
checks.command: dumpsys netstats 2>/dev/null | grep -oE 'rxBytes=[0-9]+' | head -1 | grep -oE '[0-9]+' | awk '{gb=$1/1073741824; if(gb>10) print "HIGH_"int(gb)"GB"; else print "NORMAL"}'
checks.description: High data usage (>10GB) may indicate data exfiltration

checks.label: Active Window Overlays
checks.description: Active window overlays may enable tapjacking or credential theft

checks.label: System Services Total Count
checks.description: Unusual number of system services (<50 or >300) may indicate tampering

checks.label: Non-Standard System Services
checks.description: Non-standard services detected - review for potential threats
